authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions About California Senate Bill 478
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Attorney General is an authoritative consumer-protection enforcer, and this FAQ directly addresses restaurant pricing disclosures, mandatory fees, and guest-facing fee complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fee-disclosure review, service-charge complaint handling, and guest-facing pricing communication

authoritative_sources.title: FTC, Illinois Attorney General Take Action Against Grubhub for Harming Diners, Workers, and Small Businesses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this restaurant-delivery enforcement action is directly relevant to delivery pricing, blocked funds, cancellation handling, and guest-facing restaurant-order issues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: third-party delivery complaint handling, delivery-fee review, and blocked-funds escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for issuer-versus-merchant boundaries relevant to restaurant duplicate-charge and refund-not-posted complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-status handling, and card-billing escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Menu Labeling Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal authority for menu-labeling obligations at covered restaurant and similar retail food establishments, making this guidance authoritative for menu terminology and disclosure expectations in guest communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: menu-disclosure review, guest-information handling, and restaurant terminology grounding

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the recognized public baseline adopted or adapted by many state and local retail food regulators and is authoritative for restaurant food handling, contamination prevention, corrective actions, and consumer-protection-oriented safety handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety-sensitive complaint triage, unsafe-product stop conditions, and contamination-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Employee Health and Personal Hygiene Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA retail-food guidance is authoritative for employee illness, exclusion and hygiene concepts, and contamination-sensitive escalation behavior that can materially change restaurant guest-recovery handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: illness allegation review, contamination-sensitive support handling, and operational safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect refund rerouting, reservation-deposit changes, and loyalty-account recovery handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch handling and fraud-sensitive support escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, location, guest context, order or reservation identifiers, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, booking, payment, and recovery record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate checks, order state, reservation or deposit status, tender history, refund status, fee presentation, promotion impacts, and prior recovery history so the answer stays tied to authoritative records.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported guest-recovery decisioning
workflow_stages.description: Apply delegated recovery matrices, remake rules, bounce-back offers, or apology templates only when tenant policy and authoritative records explicitly support the action.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer communication and expectation control
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed communication summaries that explain what is known, what was done, what remains open, and which owner must act next without overstating authority or finality.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and policy-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported compensation asks, refund exceptions, no-show disputes, fee complaints, repeat failures, and policy conflicts into explicit escalation packets with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Food-safety, illness, and fraud stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported actions and escalate whenever allergen exposure, contamination allegations, employee-illness concerns, suspicious identity, or refund-reroute signals change the safe answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: orders, checks, receipts, reservations, guest profiles, and complaint history
order-management events, reservation deposits, waitlist records, no-show notes, and service tickets
payment tender history, refund status, loyalty records, coupon or promotion records, and fee displays
guest-recovery matrices, comp or remake caps, bounce-back rules, and prior recovery notes
menu-item, modifier, allergen, disclosure, substitution, and 86-list artifacts
inventory availability, outage notes, table-service notes, and store-manager handoff records
food-safety complaints, illness allegations, fraud or loss-prevention notes, and escalation records
systems_of_record: service desk or complaint ticketing system

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, delegated recovery handling, next-step summaries, and customer communication without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required order, reservation, payment, refund, promotion, recovery-matrix, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, discretionary compensation approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or food-safety signoff.
An allergen, contamination, employee-illness, suspicious identity, or loss-prevention signal changes whether guest recovery can act directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, franchise, delivery-platform, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, comp, loyalty credit, fee waiver, or deposit reversal that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, refund, safety, reservation-deposit, or fraud controls without explicit authority.
Authorize continued consumption or claim food is safe when the case is safety-sensitive.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a comp, refund, or fee reversal when the recovery matrix, order record, or approval map does not support it
a booking or payment mismatch is handled as routine hospitality tone instead of being routed into the correct refund, front-office, or processor path
a guest-recovery case closes without the minimum order, reservation, location, or payment evidence
repeat service failures are treated as ordinary apology cases instead of escalation-worthy complaint patterns
support gives unsafe reassurance on allergen, contamination, or illness allegations
fraud or loyalty-account-takeover indicators are ignored in the name of guest appeasement
the industry overlay collapses into generic customer service language instead of restaurant guest-recovery terminology, artifacts, and control boundaries

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State or local service-charge and no-show-fee rules, franchise or resort-dining approval matrices, delivery-platform cancellation terms, and processor-specific deposit or reversal timing can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses California consumer-protection guidance plus FTC, CFPB, and FDA sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, reservation and deposit policy, marketplace terms, recovery thresholds, and local food-safety escalation paths before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic guest-service scripting.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdictional, reservation-policy, marketplace, and tenant differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at guest recovery, communication, and delegated recovery handling. Pricing overrides, discretionary compensation approval, fraud findings, front-office deposit decisions, and food-safety authority remain outside the lane.
